definition: Post-2010 EM FX crises have shifted to originate predominantly from commodity exporters (4/7: Russia 2014, Nigeria 2015-16, Angola 2018, Colombia/Chile 2022) versus the 1990s pattern of importer crises (Mexico 94, Asia 97-98, Brazil 99). The Dutch Disease mechanism is well-specified but the sample (n=7) may be too small to confirm a structural shift versus the 2014-16 oil collapse dominating.
evidence: 4/7 post-2010 crises originated in commodity exporters
Sample potentially dominated by 2014-16 oil collapse
open_questions: Is this a genuine structural shift or artifact of the 2014-16 oil price collapse?
Does the shift change optimal early warning indicators?
How does the commodity exporter pattern interact with fiscal-monetary conflict rotation?
